text: The patient cart of the surgical robot is shown with robotic arms docked to the ports in the patient's body. The robotic arms carry surgical instruments, and the sterile interface is maintained by camera arm drapes and instrument arm drapes.

The patient cart is positioned from the right shoulder of the patient. Robotic arm one is placed in the left hypochondrium just below the left costal margin using an 8mm metallic Da Vinci port with a blunt obturator, under visual guidance.
The insufflated abdomen is visible after pneumoperitoneum creation, with the camera port inserted above the umbilicus.
The surgeon inserts a 5mm trocar, designated as the third assistant or retraction port, into the right flank of the patient.
The robotic cart is docked to the established ports in the insufflated abdomen, allowing the surgeon to begin the procedure from the master console.
The assistant uses the retraction port to pull the gallbladder fundus towards the right diaphragm, facilitating exposure of the surgical field.
The surgeon utilizes an endorhist hook in robotic arm one and fenestrated bipolar forceps in robotic arm two, manipulating these instruments within the surgical field.
The robotic hook is demonstrated being used as a right angle dissector, cutting instrument, and dissecting instrument, showcasing its versatility and range of motion during the procedure.

The surgeon completes the anterior and posterior dissection, creating a window behind the cystic duct. The critical view of safety is achieved, revealing the dissected cystic artery, cystic duct, and the liver surface.
The surgeon uses endoristed instruments to dissect the gallbladder, achieving the critical view of safety with precise manipulation.
The surgeon uses a hemolog clip applier, an endoristed instrument, to apply clips to the cystic duct and artery, preparing to divide these structures.
The surgeon uses routine laparoscopic scissors to divide the cystic duct and artery, preparing to dissect the gallbladder from the liver surface.
The gallbladder is placed into an endobag and retrieved through the umbilical trocar. The robot is undocked, and a 5mm scope is inserted through the left hypochondrial trocar for visualization.
